title: revise-pr: post-publish PR feedback ingestion skill

content.title: revise-pr: post-publish PR feedback ingestion skill
content.summary: Designed a new ingestion skill `revise-pr` that processes reviewer and bugbot feedback on already-published PRs. The grill session resolved all judgment calls. The skill follows the ingestion/action architectural split: harvest PR comments via GitHub GraphQL, run provisional classification, apply a mandatory universal diligence gate (the only untrusted data inlet in the system), present an HITL confirmation gate, fire attribution-tracer only for confirmed escapes (after the HITL gate), then stop at a verified seed. Three companion stubs are defined: `vet` (diligence executor, manual for v1), `relay` (reply egress, notify-only), and `investigate` (future standalone external-signal explorer). The seed schema gets an optional provenance block. All decisions are resolved; seed is ready for tasking.
decisions: Skill name is `revise-pr`. Scope: user's own already-published PR, post-review feedback only. Explicitly not for pre-vetting own PRs (code-review does that) and not for reviewing others' PRs (code-review does that).

Ingestion is dual-mode: PR URL primary (`gh pr view <url>`); fallback to `gh pr list --author @me --state open` in cwd when no URL given. Asserts cwd repo == PR repo and bails with a clear message if not. No hardcoded repo — operates on the repo in cwd.

Harvest is wide across all surfaces: inline review threads, review summary bodies (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT), top-level conversation comments, and bugbot. Uses `gh api graphql` to get `isResolved` flag, which is not available on REST. Filter at harvest: unresolved threads + bugbot (first-class source by known bot login) + non-self-authored. Same gh auth token and scopes as existing code-review skill; no new infra or permissions required.

Triage uses a two-axis model. Axis 1 — classification: `bug` / `change` / `question` / `diligence` / `discuss` / `nit`. Axis 2 — escape-disposition (bug-class only): `confirmed_escape` / `false_flag` / `not_an_escape` / `unverified` (skeptical default). Skill auto-classifies provisionally then presents a triage table for HITL confirmation before any action.

Diligence gate is mandatory and universal: every harvested item must pass diligence before categorization finalizes. `revise-pr` is the only untrusted data inlet in the pipeline. Other ingestion skills (debug, grill-me, code-review) are pre-verified by a human or a ruleset before entering the pipeline; this one is not. Diligence runs in both directions — catches a false flag dressed as a bug and promotes a real bug dressed as a question. Executor is manual-you for v1; the `vet` skill slots into the same gate when built.
HITL confirmation gate runs after diligence: user confirms or overrides the evidence-backed final categorization before any action proceeds.

Attribution-tracer fires at step 6 of the pipeline (the act step), after the HITL confirmation gate. The full pipeline order is: (1) harvest, (2) provisional categorize, (3) diligence ALL, (4) reconcile, (5) HITL gate, (6) act — fire attribution-tracer once per `confirmed_escape` item. The PR comment is the artifact evidence. This is the only point in the pipeline where PR-caught escapes can be captured; nothing downstream fires attribution-tracer for these items. Attribution demotes to candidate/improves:null when the escape is ambiguous.

Skill boundary is the seed. Full pipeline for this skill: harvest → provisional triage → diligence → HITL gate → invoke `/to-seed` (adversary panel verifies) → stop. Skill does not drive to-tasks, run-tasks, branch checkout, or push. What the downstream pipeline does with the seed is outside this skill's concern.

Seed schema gets an optional structured `provenance` block: `{pr_url, head_branch, items[{thread_node_id, final_class, disposition}]}`. Additive and optional; all other seed producers (grill-me, prototype, to-seed) are unaffected.
Non-code items (question/discuss/nit) are captured in `provenance.items[]` and printed to the user via a notify-only `relay` stub. No GitHub posting occurs in v1.

`revise-pr` gets a node in `skill-pipeline.json` with edges to `to-tasks` and the `relay` stub. Managed learning-capture tail is injected via `inject-learning-tail.py`. All wiring follows the standard DAG/non-blocking-suggestion model.

`revise-pr` is an ingestion skill following the ingestion/action architectural split. All ingestion paths should converge on the seed (the universal IR with adversary verification). `debug` and `code-review` currently skip the seed/verification — that is an architectural gap in those skills, not a pattern to follow.

Three companion skills are stubbed today: `vet` (diligence executor for verifying PR claims against code), `relay` (reply egress, notify-only v1), and `investigate` (future standalone external-signal explorer for Sentry/Slack/Datadog). These stubs exist as wiring anchors in `skill-pipeline.json`; none are functionally implemented. `investigate` is a separate standalone skill not specifically tied to `revise-pr`.
`provenance-schema.json` producer enum needs a new entry `revise-pr` to make it a valid task producer when the skill eventually emits tasks via to-tasks.

disposed_threads.text: Should the skill write tasks JSON directly like debug, skipping the seed pipeline?
Should to-tasks need a branch-override input to honor the existing PR branch?
Should vet/investigate be built as full skills as part of this effort?
Does GraphQL require separate auth configuration beyond existing gh setup?
Should non-code items trigger a full respond/relay skill with GitHub posting in v1?

deferred.text: Typed-task / pluggable-executor generalization of to-tasks and run-tasks
deferred.rationale: Would allow a `task_type` key on tasks so run-tasks dispatches to different executors (code → TDD, respond → writer skill, doc → Notion updater). Enables `relay` to generate reply-tasks inside the same pipeline. Not needed for v1 of revise-pr.
deferred.context: User raised this when considering how non-code reply tasks would eventually fit the same pipeline. The relay stub is the current placeholder. When task_type is added, relay becomes a real task-generating executor rather than a notify-only stub.

deferred.rationale: Name `code-review` collides with `caveman-review` and `frontend-review`. Scope may need re-evaluation given the ingestion/action split. Not blocking revise-pr.
deferred.context: Collision noted during naming discussion. User is not sure whether to keep, rename, or retire the skill. Needs its own dedicated session.

deferred.text: Build `vet` as a full functional skill
deferred.rationale: v1 of revise-pr uses manual diligence at the gate. `vet` would automate: take a flagged claim + code pointers, verify against actual code, return confirmed/false_flag/not_an_escape verdict. Stubbed today.
deferred.context: `vet` is the diligence executor that slots into the mandatory gate inside revise-pr. It has reuse potential beyond PR review (any code claim that needs verification). Build separately when manual diligence proves too slow.

deferred.text: Build `relay` as a full functional skill
deferred.rationale: v1 relay is notify-only (prints non-code items to user). Full relay posts threaded GitHub replies, resolves threads, and requires careful authorization gate for outward-facing writes.
deferred.context: Outward-facing posting is irreversible and requires explicit authorization. Deferred until the write-back UX and error cases are properly designed. May benefit from the typed-task generalization to generate reply tasks.

deferred.text: Build `investigate` as a full functional skill
deferred.rationale: Standalone external-signal explorer: makes sense of Sentry tickets, Slack threads, Datadog signals, open questions without context. Separate from `vet` (which verifies flagged code claims).
deferred.context: User described a need for a skill to pass in a Sentry ticket or Slack conversation and investigate it. Methodology is similar to `vet` but different domain, different exit. Both belong to an investigation skill family.

content.next_action: Run /to-tasks to generate the implementation task breakdown for revise-pr
content.problem_statement: After publishing a PR, reviewer and bugbot feedback arrives as untrusted, mixed-quality data (real bugs, hallucinations, questions, nits) with no verified pipeline to triage, verify, and route it into the existing trusted ingestion/action architecture.
content.solution: A new `revise-pr` ingestion skill harvests all PR feedback via GitHub GraphQL, runs provisional classification followed by mandatory universal diligence (the only untrusted inlet), presents an HITL gate, fires attribution-tracer for confirmed escapes (after the HITL gate), and stops at a verified seed for downstream action via the standard pipeline.
implementation_decisions: `gh api graphql` for harvesting: REST `gh api .../pulls/{n}/comments` does not expose `isResolved`; GraphQL `reviewThreads` does. Same gh binary, same OAuth token, same scopes as code-review.

Harvest filter: `isResolved === false` (GraphQL), exclude comments where `author.login` matches authenticated user, include bugbot by known bot login as first-class source (not filtered at harvest; noise-filtering is at triage).
Provisional triage table presented to user after auto-classification; the table is a proposal only. User may override any class before diligence runs.

Diligence gate surfaces: flagged claim text + originating comment URL + relevant code context side by side for each item. User renders the verdict (v1). `vet` skill slots in at the same gate when built.

Attribution invocation: `subagent_type: attribution-tracer`, inputs: `issue_description` (confirmed defect text), `pr_url`, `branch` (PR head branch), `transcript_path`. Fires once per `confirmed_escape` item after HITL gate, not once per session.
`seed.provenance` block shape: `{pr_url: string, head_branch: string, items: Array<{thread_node_id: string, final_class: string, disposition: string}>}`.
`relay` stub v1 behavior: read `seed.provenance.items[]`, filter to non-code classes (question/discuss/nit), print formatted list to user. No external calls, no GitHub posting.
SSO 403 handling: detect `Resource not accessible by integration` error on GraphQL call and print exact `gh auth refresh -h <hostname>` recovery command.
testing_decisions: Attribution fires only on `confirmed_escape`; false_flag, not_an_escape, and unverified items never trigger attribution-tracer.
Diligence gate is non-optional; no code path may bypass it. Every item exits diligence with an explicit verdict before HITL gate.
Skill never makes outward HTTP writes (no comment posting, no thread resolution) during its own run.
Repo assertion (cwd repo == PR repo) must fail loudly with a clear message, not silently operate on the wrong repo.

out_of_scope.rationale: That is the scope of the existing `code-review` skill. revise-pr is post-publish only.
That is the scope of the existing `code-review` skill. revise-pr only processes feedback on the user's own published work.

out_of_scope.text: Driving code execution (run-tasks, tdd) from within revise-pr
out_of_scope.rationale: revise-pr is an ingestion skill that stops at the seed. Execution is downstream and outside its concern.

out_of_scope.text: Posting or writing replies/comments to GitHub directly from revise-pr
out_of_scope.rationale: Outward-facing writes are irreversible and require deliberate separate authorization. Deferred to the `relay` skill.

out_of_scope.text: Direct-write tasks JSON (debug-style, skipping the seed pipeline)
out_of_scope.rationale: Explicitly rejected. The seed pipeline enforces adversary verification which is required for untrusted data. Skipping it would bypass the one safety gate that makes the corpus trustworthy.

risks_and_tradeoffs: Diligence is manual in v1; human error at the gate can still let false escapes into the attribution corpus until `vet` is built.

Attribution fires before the fix exists (after the HITL gate but before downstream execution). The `fix` field in the attribution record will be empty/TBD until a downstream session confirms a fix. This is intentional — escape attribution is about why the defect got past the vet, not what the fix is.

GraphQL org SSO: a token that passes `gh pr diff` may 403 on `gh api graphql` for private repos behind SSO. First-run detection and the `gh auth refresh` recovery message handle this; it is a one-time setup cost, not a recurring block.

Bugbot hallucinations and low-context reviewer comments will be common in practice. The diligence gate is the only structural defense; its quality depends on the user's attention during the manual v1 pass.
further_notes: The ingestion/action split is the key architectural principle `revise-pr` embodies. `debug` and `code-review` currently violate it by skipping the seed/verification step. This is a candidate for future refactoring of those skills.

`vet` and `investigate` belong to an investigation skill family with shared methodology (read inputs, reason about them, render a verdict) but distinct domains: `vet` verifies flagged code claims, `investigate` explores unknown external signals.
The `relay` stub today wires the egress slot in the pipeline DAG. When the typed-task generalization lands, `relay` can become a task-generating executor rather than a notify-only printer.
Temp files /tmp/seed-1ae70626-4156-43dd-9509-b60c6cef98df.json and /tmp/cleaned-transcript-1ae70626-4156-43dd-9509-b60c6cef98df.jsonl need manual cleanup (rm blocked by hook).

tasks.title: Scaffold revise-pr SKILL.md with harvest stage
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skills/revise-pr/SKILL.md with frontmatter (name, description, slug, model, effort) and the full harvest stage. Step 1 — input: accept a PR URL argument; when no URL given, fallback to `gh pr list --author @me --state open` in cwd and prompt user to pick. Step 2 — repo assertion: resolve the PR's repo from `gh pr view <url> --json headRepository` and compare to the cwd repo from `git remote get-url origin`; bail immediately with a clear error message if they differ. Step 3 — GraphQL harvest: call `gh api graphql` to fetch all review threads via `reviewThreads(first:100)` (capturing `isResolved`, `comments.nodes[].{body,author.login,url,databaseId}`), all review summary bodies (APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT), and top-level pull request comments. Apply harvest filter: keep only threads where `isResolved === false`, exclude comments where `author.login` matches the authenticated user (from `gh api user`), and include bugbot comments by known bot login as a first-class source regardless of other filters. Detect `Resource not accessible by integration` error on GraphQL response and print the exact recovery command `gh auth refresh -h <hostname>` before stopping. All behavior is via inline skill instructions; no separate helper scripts needed for this slice.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md exists at the correct path with valid frontmatter (name, description, slug, model, effort fields present)
Skill instructions describe the fallback `gh pr list --author @me --state open` flow when no PR URL argument is given
Skill instructions describe the repo assertion check and state that a mismatch prints a clear error and stops immediately
Skill instructions include the complete GraphQL query structure for reviewThreads, review bodies, and PR comments
Harvest filter logic is stated explicitly: isResolved===false, exclude self-authored, include bugbot by login
SSO 403 handling is described: detect 'Resource not accessible by integration' and print `gh auth refresh -h <hostname>` recovery command
A synthetic dry-run instruction set (run skill on a real or test PR) produces a harvested item list with correct structure

tasks.title: Add provisional triage stage to revise-pr SKILL.md
tasks.description: Extend SKILL.md with the provisional triage stage (pipeline step 2). After harvest output is displayed, the skill auto-classifies each item using a two-axis model. Axis 1 — class: one of `bug` / `change` / `question` / `diligence` / `discuss` / `nit`. Axis 2 — escape-disposition (bug class only): `confirmed_escape` / `false_flag` / `not_an_escape` / `unverified` (skeptical default; always start here). Present a triage table to the user with columns: item number, thread/comment excerpt, proposed class, proposed disposition (bug class only). Instruct the skill to accept user overrides for any row before proceeding to diligence. State that the table is a proposal only and that no action is taken until the HITL gate.
acceptance_criteria: Skill instructions describe the two-axis classification model with all valid class values and all valid disposition values
Triage table format is described with at minimum: item number, excerpt, proposed class, proposed disposition columns
Escape-disposition column appears only for bug-class items
Instructions state that `unverified` is the skeptical default for escape-disposition (not `confirmed_escape`)
Instructions state the triage table is a proposal and user may override any row before diligence runs
Instructions state that no action fires until the HITL gate in a later step

tasks.title: Add diligence gate to revise-pr SKILL.md
tasks.description: Extend SKILL.md with the mandatory universal diligence gate (pipeline step 3). The gate is non-optional: every harvested item must pass through it with no bypass path. For each item, surface three pieces of context side by side: (1) the flagged claim text verbatim, (2) the originating comment URL, (3) the relevant code context from the PR diff or referenced files. The user renders a verdict for each item in v1 (manual diligence). Valid verdicts: `confirmed_escape`, `false_flag`, `not_an_escape`, or `unverified`. Reconcile: diligence may promote an item (e.g. a `question` that turns out to be a real bug) or demote it (e.g. a `bug` that is a false flag). Instructions must state that no item may exit the gate without an explicit verdict — partial completion is not allowed. Note that the `vet` skill slots into this gate in a future version.
acceptance_criteria: Instructions state explicitly that the gate is mandatory and every item must pass through it — no bypass path
Instructions describe the three context surfaces per item: claim text, comment URL, relevant code context
All four valid diligence verdicts are listed: confirmed_escape, false_flag, not_an_escape, unverified
Instructions state that a promoted or demoted classification after diligence is valid and should update the triage table
Instructions state that partial completion (some items not reviewed) is not allowed before proceeding to the HITL gate
Instructions note that `vet` skill slots into the same gate when built

tasks.title: Add HITL confirmation gate + attribution-tracer invocation to revise-pr SKILL.md
tasks.description: Extend SKILL.md with pipeline steps 5 and 6. Step 5 — HITL confirmation gate: present the evidence-backed final categorization (post-diligence) as a confirmation table. User confirms or overrides each item's final class and disposition before any action proceeds. This is the last human checkpoint before actions fire. Step 6 — attribution-tracer invocation: after the HITL gate, for each item whose final disposition is `confirmed_escape`, spawn one `attribution-tracer` subagent with inputs: `issue_description` (the confirmed defect claim text), `pr_url` (the PR URL from Step 1), `branch` (the PR head branch from `gh pr view --json headRefName`), `transcript_path` (absolute path to current session transcript). Attribution fires once per `confirmed_escape` item, not once per session. Items with disposition `false_flag`, `not_an_escape`, or `unverified` must never trigger attribution-tracer. The `fix` field in attribution records will be empty/TBD since no fix exists yet; this is intentional. Attribution demotes to candidate/improves:null when the escape is ambiguous. Include the invariant explicitly: skill never makes outward HTTP writes (no GitHub comment posting, no thread resolution) during its own run.
acceptance_criteria: Instructions describe the HITL confirmation table and state it is the last checkpoint before any action fires
Instructions state attribution-tracer fires once per confirmed_escape item, not once per session
Instructions list the exact subagent inputs: issue_description, pr_url, branch, transcript_path
Instructions explicitly state that false_flag, not_an_escape, and unverified items never trigger attribution-tracer
Instructions note that the fix field will be empty/TBD and that this is intentional
Instructions state the skill never makes outward HTTP writes during its own run

tasks.title: Add seed stop + provenance block + relay behavior + learning-capture tail to revise-pr SKILL.md
tasks.description: Extend SKILL.md with pipeline step 7 and the closing blocks. Step 7 — seed stop: after attribution-tracer runs for all confirmed_escape items, invoke `/to-seed` passing the session context including a structured provenance block. Build the provenance block as: `{pr_url: string, head_branch: string, items: Array<{thread_node_id: string, final_class: string, disposition: string}>}`. Skill stops at the seed — it does not invoke /to-tasks, /run-tasks, branch checkout, or push. State this boundary explicitly. Relay stub behavior (inline v1): after /to-seed completes, read `seed.provenance.items[]`, filter to non-code classes (question, discuss, nit), and print a formatted list to the user. No external calls or GitHub posting from this step. Add the standard managed learning-capture tail block (matching the pattern used in other SKILL.md files) with skill slug `revise-pr` and supported triggers. The final closing section of the skill should list next-step suggestions: /to-tasks when the seed is ready for implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Provenance block shape is specified exactly: {pr_url, head_branch, items[{thread_node_id, final_class, disposition}]}
Instructions state explicitly that the skill stops after /to-seed and does not invoke /to-tasks, /run-tasks, branch checkout, or push
Relay stub section filters to question/discuss/nit classes only and states no external calls are made
Learning-capture tail block is present with correct skill slug 'revise-pr'
Next-step suggestion '/to-tasks' appears in the closing section
Full SKILL.md is reviewed end-to-end and all seven pipeline steps are present in correct order

tasks.title: Wire revise-pr into schema, pipeline, and skill registry
tasks.description: Three infrastructure changes plus skill registration. (1) Schema: add `revise-pr` to the `producer` enum in ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/contracts/provenance-schema.json. (2) Pipeline: add a `revise-pr` node to ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/skill-pipeline.json with edges `{skill:'to-tasks', when:'seed is verified and ready for implementation'}` and `{skill:'relay', when:'non-code items need user notification'}`. Also add stub nodes for `vet` (next:[]), `relay` (next:[]), and `investigate` (next:[]) — these are wiring anchors; none are functionally implemented. (3) Stub skill directories: create minimal SKILL.md stub files at skills/vet/SKILL.md, skills/relay/SKILL.md, and skills/investigate/SKILL.md — each with a frontmatter header and a one-line description stating it is a stub. These are required because inject-learning-tail.py validates that every slug referenced in pipeline 'next' edges has a corresponding directory under claude-code-shared/skills/. (4) Run: `python ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/inject-learning-tail.py revise-pr` (or equivalent invocation) to bake the closing next-step suggestion into SKILL.md from the pipeline graph. (5) Run `/register-skill` to add revise-pr to the skill registry (settings.json). Validate each change: run `bash ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh --instance ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/contracts/provenance-schema.json /dev/null` to verify the schema still parses; confirm skill-pipeline.json is valid JSON with `python3 -c 'import json,sys; json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))' skill-pipeline.json`.
acceptance_criteria: 'revise-pr' appears in the producer enum of provenance-schema.json
validate-schema.sh exits 0 after the schema change
skill-pipeline.json is valid JSON after additions (python3 json.load check passes)
revise-pr node exists in skill-pipeline.json with edges to to-tasks and relay
vet, relay, and investigate stub nodes exist in skill-pipeline.json with next:[]
Stub SKILL.md files exist for vet, relay, and investigate under claude-code-shared/skills/
inject-learning-tail.py completes without error on revise-pr SKILL.md
revise-pr appears in the skill registry after /register-skill runs
